title: Easy Light Filipino Rice Flour Pancakes
description: A traditional Filipino dish featuring light and fluffy rice flour pancakes, this recipe is easy to make and perfect for a snack or breakfast, it is also satvik friendly and cooked to perfection in a paella pan with steam.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the rice flour and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the water, eggs, and coconut milk.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined.
Heat the paella pan over medium heat and brush with melted butter.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter into the pan.
Cook the pancakes for 2-3 minutes, until bubbles appear on the surface and the edges start to dry.
Loosen the pancakes with a spatula and flip them over, cooking for another 1-2 minutes, until golden brown.
Repeat the process until all the batter is used up, adjusting the heat as needed to prevent burning.
Serve the pancakes hot, garnished with chopped green onions and a drizzle of coconut milk if desired.
